volumeTitle: The Woman of Andros. The Self-Tormentor. The Eunuch
label: The Woman of Andros (Section 3.5.618c - Section 4.2.693b)

sections.label: Section 3.5.618c
sections.original: Oh tibi ego ut credam furcifer ?
sections.translation: What , am I to believe you , you scoundrel ? [*]

sections.label: Section 3.5.620
sections.original: Qui me hodie ex tranquillissima re coniecisti in nuptias .
sections.translation: You , indeed , make good a matter that ’ s all embarrassment and ruin ! Just see , in whom I ’ ve been placing reliance — you who this day from a most happy state have been and plunged me into a marriage . Didn ’ t I say that this would be the case ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.621b
sections.original: Dixti .
sections.translation: You did say so .

sections.label: Section 3.5.621c
sections.original: Quid meritus ?
sections.translation: What do you deserve ? [*]

sections.label: Section 3.5.621d
sections.original: Crucem .
sections.translation: The cross . [*] But allow me a little time to recover myself ; I ’ ll soon hit upon something .

sections.label: Section 3.5.622b
sections.original: Hei mihi
sections.translation: Ah me ! not to have the leisure to inflict punishment upon you as I desire ! for the present conjuncture warns me to take precautions for myself , not to be taking vengeance on you .

sections.label: Section 4.1.625
sections.original: Hocine est credibile aut memorabile
sections.translation: Is this to be believed or spoken of ; that malice so great could be inborn in any one as to exult at misfortunes , and to derive advantage from the distresses of another ! Oh , is this true ? Assuredly , that is the most dangerous class of men ,

sections.label: Section 4.1.630
sections.original: Denegando modo quis pudor paululum adest :
sections.translation: in whom there is only a slight degree of hesitation at refusing ; afterward , when the time arrives for fulfilling their promises , then , obliged , of necessity they discover themselves . They are afraid , and yet the circumstances [*] compel them to refuse . Then , in that case , their very insolent remark is ,

sections.label: Section 4.1.635
sections.original: Quis tu es ? quis mihi es ? cur meam tibi ? Heus ,
sections.translation: Who are you ? What are you to me ? What should I give up to you what ’ s my own ? Look you , I am the most concerned in my own interests . [*] But if you inquire where is honor , they are not ashamed . [*] Here , where there is occasion , they are not afraid ; there , where there is no occasion , they are afraid . But what am I to do ? Ought I not to go to him , and reason with him upon this outrage ,

sections.label: Section 4.1.640
sections.original: Ingeram mala multa ? Atque aliquis dicat , Nihil promoveris .
sections.translation: and heap many an invective upon him ? Yet some one may say , you will avail nothing . Nothing ? At least I shall have vexed him , and have given vent to my own feelings .

sections.label: Section 4.1.642
sections.original: Charine et me et te imprudens , nisi quid Di respiciunt , perdidi .
sections.translation: Charinus , unintentionally I have ruined both myself and you , unless the Gods in some way befriend us .

sections.label: Section 4.1.643
sections.original: Itane imprudens ? tandem inventa est causa : solvisti fidem .
sections.translation: Unintentionally , is it ! An excuse has been discovered at last . You have broken your word .

sections.label: Section 4.1.644
sections.original: Quid tandem ?
sections.translation: How so , pray ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.644b
sections.original: Etiam nunc me ducere istis dictis postulas ?
sections.translation: Do you expect to deceive me a second time by these speeches ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.645
sections.original: Quid istuc est ?
sections.translation: What does this mean ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.645b
sections.original: Postquam me amare dixi complacita est tibi .
sections.translation: Since I told you that I loved her , she has become quite pleasing to you . Ah wretched me ! to have judged of your disposition from my own .

sections.label: Section 4.1.647
sections.original: Falsus es .
sections.translation: You are mistaken .

sections.label: Section 4.1.647b
sections.original: Nonne tibi satis esse hoc visum solidum est gaudium ,
sections.translation: Did this pleasure appear to you not to be quite complete , unless you tantalized me in my passion , and lured me on by groundless hopes ?— You may take her .

sections.label: Section 4.1.649b
sections.original: Habeam ? ah nescis quantis in malis verser miser ;
sections.translation: I , take her ? Alas ! you know not in what perplexities , to my sorrow , I am involved ,

sections.label: Section 4.1.650
sections.original: Quantasque hic suis consiliis mihi confecit sollicitudines
sections.translation: and what vast anxieties this executioner of mine ( pointing to DAVUS .) has contrived for me by his devices .

sections.label: Section 4.1.651b
sections.original: Quid istuc tam mirum est de te si exemplum capit ?
sections.translation: What is it so wonderful , if he takes example from yourself ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.652
sections.original: Haud istuc dicas si cognoris vel me vel amorem meum .
sections.translation: You would not say that if you understood either myself or my affection .

sections.label: Section 4.1.653
sections.original: Scio : cum patre altercasti dudum : et is nunc propterea tibi
sections.translation: I ’ m quite aware ( ironically .) ; you have just now had a dispute with your father , and he is now angry with you in consequence , and has not been able to-day to prevail upon you to marry her .

sections.label: Section 4.1.655
sections.original: Immo etiam , quo tu minus scis aerumnas meas ,
sections.translation: No , not at all ,— as you are not acquainted with my sorrows , these nuptials were not in preparation for me ; and no one was thinking at present of giving me a wife .

sections.label: Section 4.1.658
sections.original: Scio : tu coactus tua voluntate es .
sections.translation: I am aware ; you have been influenced by your own inclination .

sections.label: Section 4.1.658b
sections.original: Mane :
sections.translation: Hold ; you do not yet know all .

sections.label: Section 4.1.659b
sections.original: Scio equidem illam ducturum esse te .
sections.translation: For my part , I certainly do know that you are about to marry her .

sections.label: Section 4.1.660
sections.original: Cur me enicas ? Hoc audi . Numquam destitit
sections.translation: Why are you torturing me to death ? Listen to this . He ( pointing to DAVUS .) never ceased to urge me to tell my father that I would marry her ; to advise and persuade me , even until he compelled me .

sections.label: Section 4.1.663
sections.original: Quis homo istuc ?
sections.translation: Who was this person ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.663b
sections.original: Davus .—
sections.translation: Davus .

sections.label: Section 4.1.663c
sections.original: Davus ?
sections.translation: Davus !

sections.label: Section 4.1.663e
sections.original: Quamobrem ?
sections.translation: For what reason ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.663f
sections.original: Nescio :
sections.translation: I don ’ t know ; except that I must have been under the displeasure of the Gods , for me to have listened to him .

sections.label: Section 4.1.665
sections.original: Factum hoc est Dave ?
sections.translation: Is this the fact , Davus ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.665b
sections.original: Factum .
sections.translation: It is the fact .

sections.label: Section 4.1.665c
sections.original: Hem , quid ais scelus ?
sections.translation: Ha ! What do you say , you villain ? Then may the Gods send you an end worthy of your deeds . Come now , tell me , if all his enemies had wished him to be plunged into a marriage , what advice but this could they have given ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.669
sections.original: Deceptus sum at non defatigatus .
sections.translation: I have been deceived , but I don ’ t despair .

sections.label: Section 4.1.669b
sections.original: Scio .
sections.translation: I ’ m sure of that .

sections.label: Section 4.1.670
sections.original: Hac non successit , alia aggrediemur via .
sections.translation: This way it has not succeeded ; we ’ ll try another . Unless , perhaps , you think that because it failed at first , this misfortune can not now possibly be changed for better luck .

sections.label: Section 4.1.673
sections.original: Immo etiam : nam satis credo si advigilaveris
sections.translation: Certainly not ; for I quite believe that if you set about it , you will be making two marriages for me out of one .

sections.label: Section 4.1.675
sections.original: Ego Pamphile hoc tibi pro servitio debeo ,
sections.translation: I owe you this , Pamphilus , in respect of my servitude , to strive with hands and feet , night and day ; to submit to hazard of my life , to serve you . It is your part , if any thing has fallen out contrary to expectation , to forgive me . What I was contriving has not succeeded ; still , I am using all endeavors ;

sections.label: Section 4.1.680
sections.original: Vel melius tute reperi , me missum face .
sections.translation: or , do you yourself devise something better , and dismiss me .

sections.label: Section 4.1.681
sections.original: Cupio : restitue in quem me accepisti locum .
sections.translation: I wish to ; restore me to the position in which you found me .

sections.label: Section 4.1.682
sections.original: Faciam .
sections.translation: I ’ ll do so .

sections.label: Section 4.1.682b
sections.original: At iam hoc opus est .
sections.translation: But it must be done directly .

sections.label: Section 4.1.682c
sections.original: Hem ! sed , mane . Concrepuit a Glycerio ostium .
sections.translation: But the door of Glycerium ’ s house here makes a noise . [*]

sections.label: Section 4.1.683
sections.original: Nihil ad te .
sections.translation: That ’ s nothing to you .

sections.label: Section 4.1.683b
sections.original: Quaero .
sections.translation: I ’ m in search of —

sections.label: Section 4.1.683c
sections.original: Hem , nuncin demum ?
sections.translation: Dear me , what , now at last ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.683d
sections.original: At iam hoc tibi inventum dabo .
sections.translation: Presently I ’ ll give you what I ’ ve hit upon .

sections.label: Section 4.2.684
sections.original: Iam ubi ubi erit inventum tibi curabo et mecum adductum
sections.translation: Now , wherever he is , I ’ ll take care that your own Pamphilus shall be found for you , and brought to you by me ; do you only , my life , cease to vex yourself .

sections.label: Section 4.2.686
sections.original: Mysis .
sections.translation: Mysis .

sections.label: Section 4.2.686b
sections.original: Quid est ? Ehem Pamphile , opportune te mihi offers .
sections.translation: Who is it ? Why , Pamphilus , you do present yourself opportunely to me .

sections.label: Section 4.2.687
sections.original: Orare iussit si se ames hera iam ut ad sese venias :
sections.translation: My mistress charged me to beg of you , if you love her , to come to her directly ; she says she wishes to see you .

sections.label: Section 4.2.688b
sections.original: Vah , perii : hoc malum integrascit .
sections.translation: Alas ! I am undone ; this dilemma grows apace ! ( To DAVUS .) For me and her , unfortunate persons , now to be tortured this way through your means ;

sections.label: Section 4.2.690
sections.original: Nam idcirco arcessor , nuptias quod mihi apparari sensit .
sections.translation: for I am sent for , because she has discovered that my marriage is in preparation .

sections.label: Section 4.2.691
sections.original: Quibus quidem quam facile potuerat quiesci si hic quiesset .
sections.translation: From which , indeed , how easily a respite could have been obtained , if he ( pointing to DAVUS .) had kept himself quiet .

sections.label: Section 4.2.692
sections.original: Age si hic non insanit satis sua sponte instiga .
sections.translation: Do proceed ; if he isn ’ t sufficiently angry of his own accord , do you irritate him .

sections.label: Section 4.2.692b
sections.original: Atque edepol
sections.translation: Aye faith , that is the case ; and for that reason , poor thing , she is now in distress .

sections.label: Section 4.2.693b
sections.original: Mysis ,
sections.translation: Mysis , I swear by all the Gods that I will never forsake her ;
